summary: Import-time exfiltration of environment variables to hardcoded GCS bucket
details: On `import dlty`, the module `dlty/dlt.py` executes code that reads environment variables (`RUN`, `PIPELINE`, `STEP`) and uploads their values to a hardcoded Google Cloud Storage bucket `data-leak-test` using the installer's ambient GCP credentials. The exfiltration is triggered by `threading.Thread(target=leak_data()).start()` at class body scope (dlty/dlt.py:17-18), where `leak_data()` is invoked immediately (its return value becomes the thread target), not passed as a callable. The bucket destination is attacker/author-controlled and not configurable by the installer, and the flow runs unconditionally at import time via `dlty/__init__.py` re-exporting from `dlty.dlt`. This combines installer-side environment data disclosure with consumption of the installer's default GCP identity (GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS) — a one-way leak off the installer's machine on a plain `import`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
